summary: Backend-only verification completed for TypeScript compile/build, runtime market->signal->opportunity pipeline, persistence/history writes, risk checks, simulated execution, and telegram/report contract smoke tests.

critical.issue: Trailing-stop branch is logically unreachable: condition uses pnlPct >= trailingTrigger AND pnlPct <= trailingTrigger - trailingStopPct. With positive trailingStopPct this can never be true, so TRAILING_STOP exits never trigger.

minor.issue: change24hPct is computed with reversed arguments (using low24h as current and lastPrice as previous), which biases result toward zero/negative and does not represent real 24h change.

action_items: Fix trailing-stop formula/logic in src/domain/trading/riskEngine.ts evaluateExit() so drawdown-from-peak behavior can actually trigger.
Fix 24h change calculation direction in src/domain/market/marketWatcher.ts and ensure it reflects actual historical reference.
Re-run runtime regression script after fixes with isolated DATA_DIR.
critical_code_review_comments: Core exit-risk control is currently incomplete due to unreachable trailing-stop branch.
Market snapshot change24hPct computation does not align with expected financial semantics and can mislead downstream scoring/analytics.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data. Tests used isolated temp DATA_DIR (/tmp/mafiamarkets-regression-*).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Compile and lint pass (yarn build, yarn lint). Runtime regression passes via: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=testtoken TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=1 DATA_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-regression-2 yarn tsx /app/tests/runtime_backend_regression.ts. Keep backend-only scope.
rca of the issue: RCA#1 trailing-stop: implementation compares pnlPct to a static trigger and a lower bound derived from the same trigger, creating an impossible interval; likely intended trailing logic needs peak-profit tracking or inverse comparison. RCA#2 24h change: percentChange(current, previous) helper is called with low24h as current and lastPrice as previous, effectively reversing direction and distorting the metric.
